title: get() uses .unwrap() on next() with only a comment as justification
description: lib.rs:129: data.into_iter().next().unwrap() — the 'len==1 proven above' comment is correct but .unwrap() in production code without a machine-checked invariant is fragile. Idiomatic Rust: use 'let Some((_, bs)) = data.into_iter().next() else { return Err(SecretError::NotFound); };' or restructure via into_values().next(). The comment cannot prevent a future refactor from breaking the invariant silently.
